channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Dodoma Mineral Testing Lab on Track for 2027 Completion
item.description: The Geological Survey of Tanzania (GST) confirmed that construction of the Dodoma mineral testing laboratory in Kizota, Dodoma, is progressing on schedule, with completion expected in September 2027. ...

item.title: ED Raids Linked to Terror Funding and Illegal Infiltration Network
item.description: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) conducted raids at 13 locations in connection with an alleged terror funding network involved in facilitating the illegal infiltration of Rohingya and Bangladeshi nati...

item.title: Marcello Hernández's ESPYs Monologue Highlights Immigration Heritage and Critiques Last Year's Host
item.description: Marcello Hernández, the host of Wednesday night’s ESPY Awards, opened his monologue with a sharp critique of last year’s host, Shane Gillis, for a controversial joke about WNBA star Caitlin Clark. Gil...

item.title: Rise of Democratic Socialists and Trump's Response
item.description: The article discusses the growing influence of young Democratic Socialists in the U.S., highlighting their grassroots movement challenging traditional politics. It contrasts their organic, anger-drive...

item.title: Waste-to-energy projects fall short of recycling goals
item.description: The article criticises waste-to-energy projects as an inadequate solution to Australia's waste management crisis. It argues that burning waste for energy is not renewable, creates emissions, and perpe...

item.title: Yobe State Battles Misuse of Therapeutic Food Funds
item.description: The Yobe State government is worried about people taking ready-to-use therapeutic food (RUTF) without proper use, which was meant for malnourished children. They invested millions with UNICEF to buy t...

item.title: Australian Government Launches AI Office and National Framework
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has unveiled plans to establish the Office of AI within his department and implement a national framework called 'Australian Standards' to regulate artificial intellige...

item.title: The Lasting Influence of Paul Dacre on British Journalism
item.description: The article explores how Paul Dacre, the former editor of the Daily Mail, has profoundly shaped the British press through his authoritarian leadership and right-wing editorial style. Dacre's career be...

item.title: Trisha Khalid Relocates to Nairobi After Lulu Hassan's Career Advice
item.description: Actress Trisha Khalid shared how celebrated filmmaker Lulu Hassan played a pivotal role in her career by advising her to move from Mombasa to Nairobi. During a TikTok Live, Trisha revealed that Lulu d...

item.title: Banyana aim for World Cup qualification after Wafcon return
item.description: After a disappointing semi-final exit at the last Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (Wafcon) in Morocco, Banyana Banyana are returning to the tournament with renewed focus and ambition. Their primary obje...

item.title: Overpass Reflect on Covering Harry Styles and Their Evolving Sound
item.description: Overpass, the Birmingham-based indie band, have discussed how their cover of Harry Styles' 'American Girls' is influencing their approach to songwriting and shaping their new musical direction. During...

item.title: Cape Town Mayor Announces Mosque as New Buyer of Good Hope Centre After Church Bid Falls Through
item.description: The mayor of Cape Town, Geordin Hill-Lewis, disclosed during a live radio interview that the Good Hope Centre, an iconic property in the city, will not be purchased by the Nigerian church that initial...

item.title: sophie cunningham's sister lindsey: basketball career and family legacy
item.description: the article highlights the achievements of lindsey cunningham, sophie cunningham's older sister, who built a notable basketball career at missouri university before transitioning to coaching and medic...

item.title: Three suspects arrested in Negros Oriental marine biologist's murder case
item.description: Three suspects were arrested in connection with the slaying of a marine biologist in Negros Oriental, according to a report by ABS-CBN News. The incident, which occurred on July 16, 2026, has sparked ...

item.title: Samuel Egube’s contributions to Lagos governance and development celebrated by elite group
item.description: Samuel Egube, the deputy chief of staff to Lagos State Governor Babajide Sanwo-Olu, is set to be honoured by the Urhobo and Isoko Elites for his significant contributions to public service and governa...

item.title: Pakistan Joins China-Led AI Governance Body as Founding Member
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister Ishaq Dar is set to finalize Pakistan's membership in the World Artificial Intelligence Cooperation Organisation (WAICO), a China-backed initiative aimed at global AI governance....

item.title: Chadema Takes Strong Stance Against Defectors to Strengthen Party Unity
item.description: Chadema, Tanzania's main opposition party, has announced new measures to address internal defections to rival parties. The party aims to curb disloyalty by implementing a 'kitanzi' strategy, which inv...

item.title: Former Essendon Player Supports Hird's Coaching Bid Amid Team Challenges
item.description: Jake Melksham, a former Essendon player and current Melbourne Demon, has publicly endorsed James Hird's bid to return as coach of the Bombers. Melksham, who played alongside Hird during his time at Es...

item.title: Tanzania's Women Face Rising Online Harassment, Calls for Safer Digital Spaces
item.description: A recent study reveals that nearly six out of ten young women in Tanzania have experienced online harassment, driving many to withdraw from social media. Conducted by WiLDAF, UNFPA, and Tanzania Commu...

item.title: US Imposes 25% Tariff on Brazil Imports Amid Trade Disputes
item.description: The United States has announced a new 25% tariff on specific imports from Brazil, effective July 22, following an investigation into trade practices. A senior US official confirmed the move, stating t...
